verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (hK2) is a secreted trypsin-like serine protease of the kallikrein family, produced by prostate epithelium and released into seminal fluid and serum where it cleaves pro-PSA. It has no TM domain, no GPI anchor, and no documented wash-resistant covalent tethering to a surface partner. Clinical hK2-directed programs (e.g., radioligand/CAR-T) exploit hK2 captured/retained in the prostate tumor microenvironment, but the protein itself is secreted and only reversibly associates with cells/matrix — failing the wash test.
